game_name: The Legend of the Mystical Ninja

design_philosophy.core_loop: Town exploration for resources/information → platforming combat gauntlet → boss fight → story progression. Dual-character cooperative mechanics enable creative puzzle-solving through piggyback movement control splitting.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Hybrid progression balancing resource acquisition (money for items, learning Judo abilities in towns) against escalating combat challenges in platforming sections. Two-character interdependence creates emergent difficulty negotiation in multiplayer.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing as a Japanese folk hero (Goemon) investigating supernatural occurrences across regional Japan, recruiting allies and acquiring martial abilities while uncovering a conspiracy involving theatrical villains.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Strongly commits to whimsical tone and structural variety: each stage splits exploration and combat into distinct phases; incorporates licensed minigames (Gradius recreation, air hockey, trivia) as currency sinks; embraces anachronistic absurdism (robotic ninja, amusement park base, mime counterfeiters). Rejects pure action focus in favor of town-based resource management and character progression.

difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based checkpointing; no mid-stage saves documented
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency through resource management: players control spending on thrown weapons (costs money per use) and bomb purchases. Two-player mode significantly reduces platforming difficulty by allowing one player to focus purely on landing jumps while the other controls attacks. Solo play presents harder timing demands.

progression.upgrade_system: Three-tier melee weapon improvements via grey cat pickups; four Judo ability levels (companion mount → screen-clear damage → flight → powerful repetitive attack) learned from NPCs in towns. Thrown weapon and bomb acquisition via currency or minigame wins.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Sequential stage clearing; each stage completion triggers story scene revealing next zone. Judo abilities gated by town visits and character training interactions.
replayability_hooks: Two-character selection with functionally different weapons encouraging alternate playthroughs
Minigame suite (Gradius recreation, air hockey, trivia, dice, lottery) for earning currency
Two-player cooperative mode creates different difficulty/strategy requirements than single-player
Resource optimization challenge (managing thrown weapon costs)

world_building_techniques: Town exploration reveals environmental context (suspicious Hyotoko dancers, recruitment propaganda)
NPC dialogue exposition (Kurobei the ninja cat explains premise, Koban directs investigation, Old Wise Man provides guidance)
Stage-to-stage cutscenes advancing simple rescue plot
Regional Japanese geography as narrative scaffolding (Oedo → Shikoku → Awaji → Yamato → Iga → Izumo progression)
Anachronistic world detail (amusement park base, clockwork ninjas, 'Miracle Transport Machine' cannon) supporting lighthearted tone

boss_design.philosophy: Thematic-comedic bosses matching stage environments and antagonist factions; bosses appear to be distinct combat encounters rather than recurring pattern learners. No adaptive AI documented.
notable_bosses: Ghost Woman / Kurobei (ninja cat, stage 1 revelation figure)
Lantern Man (leader of evil Hyotoko dancers)
Otafu Army leaders (mime and clown counterfeiters)
Sasuke (robotic clockwork ninja, Old Wise Man's palace guard, final major encounter)

faction_indicators: Otafu Army (clown/mime counterfeiters, amusement park operators, primary antagonists)
Hyotoko dancers (suspicious townspeople alignment, Lantern Man faction)
danger_signals: Ghost animals emerging from temple at night
environmental_storytelling: Temple with supernatural manifestations (stage 1 investigation hook)
Festival town with suspicious dancer presence (stage 2 social disruption)
Anachronistic amusement park as cover for criminal base (stage 3 tonal incongruity)
Yamato town showing recruitment propaganda (stage 4 infiltration)
Robotic palace in Iga (stage 5 technological escalation)
